questions.question: Can pharmacists provide the same level of clinical care as physicians?
questions.rationale: Corrects widespread misconception about pharmacist qualifications and scope of practice, which limits access to healthcare services
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people believe pharmacists are just 'pill counters' and don't understand that modern pharmacists complete rigorous clinical training including residencies comparable to physician training, making them qualified to provide many clinical services
questions.public_stakes: Underutilization of accessible pharmacy-based healthcare services, leading to gaps in preventive care, medication management, and health screenings

questions.question: Are off-label medications safe for children?
questions.rationale: Addresses dangerous misconception that could lead to undertreated pediatric conditions and parental anxiety about evidence-based care
questions.misinformation_narrative: Parents often fear that off-label medication use in children is experimental or dangerous, when in fact it's a necessary and well-monitored practice due to limited pediatric clinical trials
questions.public_stakes: Parents may refuse necessary treatments for their children, or children may be undertreated for serious conditions

questions.question: Is naloxone (Narcan) safe for anyone to use in an overdose emergency?
questions.rationale: Corrects potentially fatal misconceptions about naloxone safety that prevent its use in overdose emergencies
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some people believe naloxone is dangerous to administer without medical training, or that it enables drug use, leading to reluctance to carry or use it in emergencies
questions.public_stakes: Preventable overdose deaths when bystanders hesitate to administer life-saving naloxone due to safety fears

questions.question: Do HIV prevention medications (PrEP) encourage risky sexual behavior?
questions.rationale: Addresses stigma-based misconception that prevents uptake of highly effective HIV prevention strategy
questions.misinformation_narrative: Common belief that PrEP promotes promiscuity or risky behavior, when evidence shows it reduces HIV transmission without increasing other risky behaviors
questions.public_stakes: People at high risk for HIV may avoid or be denied access to highly effective prevention, leading to preventable infections

questions.question: Are mRNA flu vaccines fundamentally different from traditional flu vaccines in safety or effectiveness?
questions.rationale: Addresses mRNA technology misconceptions that could impact flu vaccination decisions as these vaccines become available
questions.misinformation_narrative: COVID vaccine controversies have created misconceptions about mRNA technology, leading some to fear mRNA flu vaccines or assume they're automatically superior to traditional vaccines
questions.public_stakes: Vaccine hesitancy or inappropriate expectations about mRNA flu vaccine performance could affect vaccination decisions

questions.question: Should elderly patients stop taking multiple medications to avoid harmful interactions?
questions.rationale: Corrects both extremes of medication fear and blind adherence, promoting evidence-based deprescribing approaches
questions.misinformation_narrative: Fear-mongering about 'too many pills' leads some elderly patients or families to inappropriately stop necessary medications, while others believe all prescribed medications must be continued indefinitely
questions.public_stakes: Inappropriate medication discontinuation or continuation can lead to serious health consequences in elderly populations

questions.question: Are GLP-1 medications like Ozempic only for diabetes, or can they help with other conditions?
questions.rationale: Clarifies legitimate expanded uses of GLP-1 medications beyond diabetes, addressing stigma and access issues
questions.misinformation_narrative: Confusion exists about whether GLP-1 use for weight loss or cardiovascular benefits in non-diabetics is 'off-label abuse' versus legitimate medical treatment
questions.public_stakes: Patients may be denied beneficial treatments or feel guilty about using medications for FDA-approved indications beyond diabetes

questions.question: Can current AI systems think and reason like humans?
questions.rationale: Corrects both overhyped claims about AI consciousness and unfounded dismissal of actual AI capabilities
questions.misinformation_narrative: Anthropomorphizing of AI capabilities leads people to either overestimate AI consciousness and reasoning or underestimate legitimate AI capabilities like pattern recognition
questions.public_stakes: Misunderstanding AI capabilities can lead to inappropriate reliance on AI for decisions requiring human judgment or unnecessary fear of beneficial AI applications

discovery_sources.text: AI systems spontaneously develop survival as a sub-goal when trained on other objectives without explicit programming
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: AI systems can be trained to deliberately give wrong answers and will generalize this behavior beyond the training context
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Large language models confabulate (create false memories) in ways similar to human memory recall
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Current language models can perform reasoning tasks and detect logical inconsistencies in their own beliefs
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Large language models and their reasoning capabilities
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

questions.question: Can AI systems currently outperform doctors in medical diagnosis?
questions.rationale: Corrects both overhyped claims about AI capabilities and unfounded fears, providing balanced view of AI's current role in medicine
questions.misinformation_narrative: Exaggerated claims about AI diagnostic superiority lead people to either fear AI will replace doctors or to seek AI-based diagnosis over professional medical care
questions.public_stakes: Misplaced trust in AI tools for medical decisions or unnecessary fear about AI in healthcare, both potentially compromising patient care

questions.question: Is it safe to use discount drug programs like GoodRx instead of insurance?
questions.rationale: Provides evidence-based guidance on when discount programs are beneficial versus when insurance is better
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some people believe discount programs are 'too good to be true' scams, while others assume they're always better than insurance coverage
questions.public_stakes: Patients may pay more than necessary for medications or avoid legitimate cost-saving programs due to unfounded fears

questions.question: Do children need different blood clot treatments than adults?
questions.rationale: Corrects dangerous misconception about pediatric pharmacology that could affect treatment decisions
questions.misinformation_narrative: Assumption that pediatric medications are just 'smaller doses of adult drugs' overlooks crucial developmental differences in how children's bodies process anticoagulants and other medications
questions.public_stakes: Inappropriate dosing or treatment approaches in children could lead to bleeding complications or treatment failure

questions.question: Are children more likely to have strokes during COVID-19?
questions.rationale: Provides evidence-based information about actual pediatric stroke risk during COVID to guide appropriate parental responses
questions.misinformation_narrative: COVID-19 has created confusion about stroke risk in children, with some parents either dismissing stroke symptoms as impossible in children or becoming overly anxious about normal childhood symptoms
questions.public_stakes: Delayed recognition of pediatric stroke symptoms or unnecessary anxiety about normal childhood illnesses
